def: (i.e. jāti+andha). For this and other compounds see s.v. jāt, 'birth,' &c.

ety: for A. جودة, inf. n. of جود 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Excellence; quickness of apprehension, intelligence; ingenuity; benignity.
s.m. An interior space, a hollow, vacancy, cavity, concavity; inside, interior.

s.m.f. A gem, jewel; a pearl; essence, matter, substance, constituent, material part (opp. to accident), absolute or essential property; skill, knowledge, accomplishment, art; excellence, worth, merit, virtue; secret nature; defects, vices; — the diversified wavy marks, streaks, or grain of a well-tempered sword; — adj. Bright, shining, glittering
subs: adj. & s.m. Marked with jauhar; — a scimitar so marked
To show what one is made of; to show to advantage
s.m. An indivisible atom; (met.) an unrivalled or unequalled person
(-kā), One's abilities or virtues to come to light; one's defects or faults to be exposed
s.m. Men of ability, merit, or worth.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوهَر (jawhar), from Arabic جَوْهَر (jawhar), from Early Classical Persian گَوهَر (gawhar), from Middle Persian gwhl (/⁠gōhr, gōhar⁠/, “substance, essence; jewel”).

def: s.m. pl. (of jauhar), Gems, jewels ( = jawāhir).
s.m. A jeweller, a seller of gems, &c.; a lapidary; a tester, appraiser (of precious stones, &c.); — adj. Relating to a jewel, essential (as a property, &c., opp. to accidental).
wety: From جَوہَر (jauhar) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. (f. jaunī), Relating to the womb or place of birth, uterine; concerning or relating to marriage or affinity; resulting through marriage; connected by the mother's side.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

ety: Prk. जवडु (by change of ḍ to n), fr. जेव, fr. S. यावत्
def: pron. Who, which, &c. = jo, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: s.m. Land cultivated alternately by rabīʻ and ḵẖarīf sowings, land in continual cultivation; land which has yielded a spring crop of wheat and barley, and is sown for another crop of different grain.
s.m. Land cultivated alternately by rabīʻ and ḵẖarīf sowings, land in continual cultivation; land which has yielded a spring crop of wheat and barley, and is sown for another crop of different grain.
s.f. A kind of smut in barley and wheat, in which the ears are empty.
s.m. A platform about ten feet high, from which watch is kept on cattle and crops.
s.f. = juʼār; junhār; junharī, qq.v.
s.f. = juʼār; junhār; junharī, qq.v.
v.t. To rail at, to scold, to abuse.
conjn. (Braj), Even if, although, supposing.
conjn. (Braj), Even if, although, supposing.
s.m. = jauṅḍā, q.v.
subs: s.m. A retired room, a vault; retirement, privacy.

def: s.m. Payment of village servants in kind.
s.m.f. Wrong-doing, injustice, oppression, violence, tyranny
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ज्वर (jvará).

def: s.m. A lofty building; a palace; a belvedere.
s.m. A coat of mail, cuirass, armour; — a gold or silver ornament worn by women on the upper arm, an armlet.
s.m. Property given to a bride at her marriage, a dower, a a nuptial present.
s.m. Youth; manhood, &c. = joban, q.v.
subs: s.m. The pride of youth, conceit; rashness

def: s.m. A nut; — nutmeg ( = jāʼephal)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جوز (jawz), from Arabic جَوْز (jawz), itself from Middle Persian 𐭢𐭥𐭦 (gōz, “walnut”). Doublet of گوز (goz).

def: s.m. The sign Gemini; — Orion.
s.m. = jāb, q.v. s.m. A muzzle (for large cattle), ox-muzzle; a net (for fruit-trees); a kind of grass.

adj. & s.m. Of the size or shape of a barley-corn; — barley, &c. ( = jau); a figure or mark (resembling a barley-corn) in the joints of the fingers ( = jau); a clove of garlic; a kind of stitch in needle-work
subs: s.m. A kind of small grub or worm
adj. Made with golden bits of the size and form of a barley-corn (a chain, &c.); sewed with the stitch termed jawā
s.f. A very small species of the yellow Myrobalan (haṛ or halela).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀽𑀤 (jūda), from Sanskrit द्यूत (dyūta, “gambling”).

def: s.f. The China rose, Hibiscus rosa sinensis
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀽𑀤 (jūda), from Sanskrit द्यूत (dyūta, “gambling”).

def: s.m. Speed, quickless, swiftness, rapidity.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑁄 (jo), from Sanskrit यः (yáḥ), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hyás, from Proto-Indo-European *Hyós.

def: s.m. Answer, reply, response (to a letter, writing, saying, or question); retort, rejoinder; anything answering to another, counterpart, facsimile, double, pair; return, reward, compensation; discharge, dismissal, congé; refusal (of an offer of marriage, &c.)
subs: s.m. The rejoinder or reply to an answer; rejoinder
s.m. A fitting or proper answer
'May your advice prevail,' a kind of benediction
To get (one's) discharge or dismissal, receive (one's) congé; — jawāb-děh, adj. & s.m. Answerable, responsible, amenable, accountable; — answerer; one called to account; a respondent, defendant
s.f. The giving an answer, answering; the being responsible, responsibility, liability; the defence (in a court of law)
(-ko), To relieve of responsibility; to exonerate
(-kī), To respond, to defend (a suit or action)

(-ko), To give an answer (to), to answer, reply; to give an account (of, -kā), to account (for); to be responsible, accountable, or amenable (for); to discharge, dismiss, disband; to leave, forsake, desert
s.m. Question and answer; conversation, dialogue; disputation, argument; contention, altercation
adj. & s.m. Ready at reply or rejoinder; — one who is ready at rejoinder; — reply, rejoinder
s.m. (Gram.) An apodosis, the complement or correlative of a condition
s.m. A flat refusal by way of answer, a categorical answer
adj. Requiring an answer; called to account
(-se), To request or call for an answer (from); to call to account
s.m. A definite or conclusive answer
(-kā), To make answer; to be responsible (for); to respond, to defend (a suit)
v.n. To be answered, to be silenced
v.n. To be discharged or dismissed ( = jawāb pānā)

s.m. A writing fixed to the winding-sheet, on the breast of the deceased, which it is intended that the angels Munkar and Nakīr (q.v.) should read in case the poor soul should be too much flurried to answer their questions
v.n. To be the counterpart (of, -kā), to correspond or agree (with); to be dismissed or discharged
s.m. A responsive or replicative particle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوَاب (jawāb), from Arabic جَوَاب (jawāb).

def: adj. & s.m. Responsive; in reply; counter; re-; — counterpart; a respondent, defendant; — a kind of bill of exchange, which is not paid till notice is received of the bill's having been taken up; — the chorus (especially in repeating a mars̤iya or elegy)
adj. Liberal, bountiful, munificent, generous, beneficent.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic جَوَاد (jawād).

def: s.f. See s.v. jawā. s.m. = jāb, q.v. s.m. A muzzle (for large cattle), ox-muzzle; a net (for fruit-trees); a kind of grass.

subs: s.m. A jewel-office; a storehouse for jewels
adj. Ornamented or bedecked with jewels, jewelled, studed with jewels.

def: s.m. pl. (of jawāhir), Jewels, gems, &c.
s.m. A dealer in jewels, a jeweller.
s.m. = jaṅwāʼī, jamāʼī, qq.v. s.f. A midwife's fee.
s.m. The red colour obtained from the lac insect (syn. altā).

adj. & s.m. Swift, fleet; — a fleet horse, a courser; — swiftness, speed, velocity.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

def: s.m. An Ionian, a Greek; a Mohammadan (sometimes applied to both the Mohammadan and European invaders of India); a foreigner, barbarian; the country of the Yavanas (sometimes applied to Bactria, Ionia, Greece, and, more recently, to Arabia)
subs: s.m. 'The town of the foreigner or Mohammadan,' the town of Jaunpūr (near Banāras)
s.f. A kind of ornament worn on the neck by women.
wety: Borrowed from English June, ultimately from Latin Iunius.

s.m. The fruit or enjoyment of youth
s.m. The season or time of youth.

or yāvajjīva (˚vat+jīv˚), adv. As long as life shall last, throughout life, during the whole of life, for life, for the rest of life.

s.m. corr. of next, q.v.
s.f. A thread stretched over the bridge of a musical instrument, beneath the strings (whereby the sound is said to be improved); the bridge of a stringed instrument (as a siṭār, &c.).
s.f. A digestive stomachic; a thing that causes food to digest.

def: s.f. Mace ( = jāwatrī).
s.f. Mace (the spice).
wety: Borrowed from Gujarati જાવંત્રી (jāvantrī) or Northwestern Indo-Aryan.

def: s.m. Permission; toleration; lawfulness, legality; validity; propriety
ex: اور جو بات نہیں ماننی ہوتی اس کے لیے کوئی جواز پیش کر دیتے ہیں دراصل یہ جواز جھوٹ پر مبنی ہوتا ہے۔
aur jo bāt nahī̃ mānnī hotī us ke lie koī javāz peś kar dete ha͠i dar asl ye javāz jhūṭ par mabnī hotā hai.
And he gives a justification for whatever he does not wish to accept, but in actual fact that justification is based on lies.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَوَاز (jawāz), from Arabic جَوَاز (jawāz).

ety: for A. جوازية, fr. jawāzī, fr. jawāz
def: s.f. Allowability; legality; propriety.

r: jāwīd, jāwed, jāwīdān, jāwedān, jāwīdānī, jāwedānī, jāwid, jāwidān, jāwidānī
def: adj. = P جاويداني jāwīdān, jāwedān, adj. & adv. = P جاه jāwīdānī, jāwedānī, s.f. See jāwid, jāwidān, & jāwidānī.
wety: From Classical Persian جاوید (jāwēd, “eternal”), from Middle Persian [script needed] (yʾwyt' /⁠jāwēd⁠/, “eternal”).

ety: Zend yavaê (dat. of yu)+tãṭ; cf. S. यावत
def: adj. Perpetual, eternal everlasting; — jāwidān, jāwidāna, adv. Perpetually, eternally, for ever, always.

r: jāwīdān, jāwedān, jāwīd, jāwed, jāwīdānī, jāwedānī, jāwid, jāwidān, jāwidānī
def: adj. & adv. = P جاويد jāwīd, jāwed, adj. = P جاه jāwīdānī, jāwedānī, s.f. See jāwid, jāwidān, & jāwidānī.

ety: Zend yavaê (dat. of yu)+tãṭ; cf. S. यावत
def: adj. Perpetual, eternal everlasting; — jāwidān, jāwidāna, adv. Perpetually, eternally, for ever, always.

ety: Zend yavaê (dat. of yu)+tãṭ; cf. S. यावत
def: adj. Perpetual, eternal everlasting; — jāwidān, jāwidāna, adv. Perpetually, eternally, for ever, always.
adv. = jāwidān; — s.f. Eternity; the next world.

r: jāwīdānī, jāwedānī, jāwīd, jāwed, jāwīdān, jāwedān, jāwid, jāwidān, jāwidānī
def: s.f. = P جاويد jāwīd, jāwed, adj. = P جاويداني jāwīdān, jāwedān, adj. & adv. See jāwid, jāwidān, & jāwidānī.

s.m. Wandering up and down, wandering about; moving or springing from side to side (as combatants or competitors in an amphitheatre or place of exercise); moving round (as a horse in a manege), coursing; — jaulān, jūlān, s.m. Fetters, irons
subs: v.n. To move or spring about, &c.; — v.t. To cause to move round, or spring about, &c.; — jawlān-kunān, adj. Moving, leaping, springing
(or jaulān-) gāh, s.f. A place of exercise (for troops or for horses); a champ-de-mars
s.f. Galloping fast; a gallop; fleetness
adv. With the feet in irons, fettered.

ety: fr. جول 'to go round or about'
def: adj. & s.m. Going about or travelling much; — one who goes about or travels much.

ety: fr. jaw, q.v.+īn = Zend aěna = S. इन्
def: adj. Made of barley.
part. adj. & s.m. Born; — a son, boy
part. adj. & s.f. Born; — a daughter, girl.
s.f. A spouse, wife, one wedded lawfully or according to the ritual
adj. & s.m. = jāyā, q.v. part. adj. & s.m. Born; — a son, boy
s.m. (Old H.) = jay, q.v.

r: jāyā, jānā, jāyā ćāhnā, jāyā karnā, gayā
def: the regular perf. part. of the verb jānā, is used only in compound verbs; e.g. jāyā ćāhnā, To wish to go; to be about to go
subs: To go habitually or constantly. (The irreg. part. gayā, 'gone,' is not so used.)

def: s.m.f. Winning, being victorious; conquest, victory, triumph; advancement, preferment, promotion; resignation; — (as an intj.) bravo! hurrah! all hail!; — s.m. A yellow species of the Phaseolus mungo; — name of one of the two gate-keepers of the Hindū paradise
subs: s.m. 'Guardian of victory'; a king, sovereign; name of several celebrated Hindū princes; — the plant Croton jamālgoṭa
jay-patr, s.m. A written account of a victory, record of a victory or triumph; a written and sealed decision in a cause or suit
s.m. 'Town of victory'; name of a province in Rājpūtānā, and of its capital
s.m. The reward of victory or triumph
s.m. A monument in commemoration of a victory, a triumphal column
s.m. The fame or renown of victory
s.m. Shout or song of victory, the exclamation jay-jay repeated (like the Io of the Greeks)
jay-jay-kārā, s.m. Triumph, exultation, cheers, rejoicings, jubilee; — jay-jay-kār karnā, To huzza, to shout; to exult, rejoice
s.f. Name of a rāginī or musical mode
s.m. 'Banner or symbol of victory'; name of the father of Tāla-jhaṅgha
s.m. A shout or cry of victory
jay-ḍhāk, s.m. A large kettle-drum (beaten as a sign of victory)
s.m. jay-mālā, s.f. Necklace or garland of victory, bays, laurels
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi जी (jī), from Prakrit 𑀚𑀻𑀅 (jīa), from Sanskrit जीव (jīva, “long live”). Cognate with Assamese জীউ (ziu).

def: s.f. Length and slenderness of neck.
s.m. (dialec.), Twice-ploughed land.
s.f. A flag, a banner; the tree Ӕschynomene sesban ( = jait).
s.m. The plant Ӕschynomene sesban, or Sesbania œgyptiaca; — a luxuriant crop; — name of a musical mode.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जित (jita). Compare Punjabi جِتّ (jitt).

def: s.m. See s.v. jay.
adj. & s.m. Conquering, subduing; — a conqueror.
adj. & s.m. Conquering, victorious, gaining a victory, winning (in a law-suit); — conqueror; winner (of a law-suit, &c.).
s.f. See s.v. jay.
s.m. See s.v. jaya, jay. part. adj. & s.m. Born; — a son, boy
adj. See s.v. jay, q.v.
= jay-vān, q.v.s.v. jay.
adj. Victorious, able to conquer.
s.f. Repayment, requital, return, retaliation, satisfaction, compensation, amends; reward, recompence; (in Gram.) an apodosis, the complement or correlative of a condition (or shart̤)
subs: s.m. A particle denoting compensation, or the complement of a condition; a conditional particle.

ety: pl. of jazīl, 'great, large'; but used by the Persians as a sing.
def: s.m. A large musket or rifle (used with a prong or rest); a wall-piece; a swivel
subs: s.m. One who fires a wall-piece or swivel.

s.f. A water-pot, water-jar; — a pile of pots filled with water and placed one over the other in order to be carried on the head; a ceremony preparative to marriage, in which pots filled with water are piled over one another, the whole is crowned with a bowl of sherbet, and the friends watch by it during the night. (To take the water-pots off the head of a divorced woman implies consent to marry her.)
s.m. A row, string, line; a string or line of captives chained together; the string or chain of buckets passed over the Persian wheel.
wety: Borrowed from English jail, from Middle English gayole, borrowed from Anglo-Norman jaiole, from Late Latin caveola, from Latin cavea (“cage”) + -ola (diminutive ending).

r: jhakolā, jhakol, jhakor, jhakorā, jhakolā denā, ko, jhakolnā
def: s.m. = H جهکول झकोल jhakol, s.f. A shaking, agitation; a shock, &c. = jhakor and jhakorā; splashing, a splash or dip (in water); a large wave ( = jhakorā)

v.t. (Braj) = jhakornā, q.v. v.t. To shake, to put in motion; to drive or beat (as wind or rain); cf. jhakolnā.

adj. (f. -ī), Bright, glittering, radiant, brilliant, splendid.
Glittering, sparkling; glitter, &c. = jhal-jhal, q.v.; — burn, smart. s.m. Radiance, glare, glitter, brilliance, splendour.

ety: jhaljhalā+ā˚ = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To shine, glare, glitter, sparkle; to burn, smart, tingle; to throb, beat; to become angry, be enraged, to fly into a passion.
s.f. = jhaljhalāhaṭ, q.v. s.m. Radiance, glare, glitter, brilliance, splendour.
s.m. A blister (cf. ćhilkā).
v.t. To cause to shine or sparkle, &c.; to brighten, polish, burnish; to flash.
s.m. Light, brightness, &c. = jhalak, q.v. s.f. Brightness, radiance, glare, glitter, sparkle, lustre, splendour, refulgence, reflection (of light).
s.f. A flash; a glance.
s.m. = jallā, q.v.; — a shower
adj. (f. -ī), Hot, pungent ( = jhāl); fiery, passionate, hot-tempered; lustful, libidinous.
s.m. A large basket.
v.n. To burn, to smart, to tingle; to become angry or enraged, to fly into a passion.
s.m. (prov.), A water-wheel, a Persian wheel (syn. rahaṭ).
s.f. A large basket.
adj. = jhallā, q.v. s.m. A large basket.

ety: jhāl˚ = Prk. झाल(इ), or झाले(इ) = S. ज्वालय(ति), caus. of rt. ज्वल्
def: v.t. To clean, to polish (plate, &c.).

ety: jhal˚ = S. व्हल(ति), rt. व्हल; cf. jhāṅtnā
def: v.t. To move (a fan, &c.) to and fro, to fan, to flap, to swing or wave.
v.t. To make hot or pungent, to season (pickles, &c.); — to solder
v.n. To be repaired or soldered, be cemented.
s.f. (dialec.) = ćhalnī, q.v. s.f. An instrument for skinning or peeling.
s.m. = jhulār, q.v. s.m. Swinging, waving, swaying from side to side; wavy motion (as of trees, water, &c.), oscillation.
s.m. A spring (of water), a well; a square bāʼolī or well of solid masonry.
s.m. (dialec.) A necklace.
s.f. A cymbal ( = jhājhar; jhāṅjh).
s.f. The price paid for soldering.
v.t. To cause to be moved to and fro (a fan); to have (one) fanned; to cause to be flapped.
s.m. A large instrument in the shape of a hoe used for excavating earth in well-sinking, &c.
s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).
s.m. & adv. = jham-jham, q.v. s.m. & adv. The sound of heavy rain; dashing, beating (of rain), steady downpour; — heavily and continuously (raining).
s.m. = jhāmā, q.v. s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).
s.m. The sight, or the appearance, of a beautiful woman.
s.m. Beating rain, a heavy shower; crash (as of breaking glass, &c.); — quickness, celerity, haste, dash, &c. ( = jhapākā, q.v.).
s.m. Concourse, crowd (especially of low people), mob.
v.n. To shine, to glitter, glisten, flash; to dance (cf. jhalaknā; ćamaknā).

ety: jhamak, q.v.+ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र+कः
def: s.m. = jhamak; jhalak.
s.m. A small whetstone (such as is used by housewives for sharpening their spindles, needles, &c.).
s.f. Marshy land, swamp ( = jhāwar, jhābar)
s.m. & adv. = jham-jham, q.v. s.m. & adv. The sound of heavy rain; dashing, beating (of rain), steady downpour; — heavily and continuously (raining).

ety: S. झं+मेला and मेल+कः; or el (pleon.) = अडो (with euph. y inserted) = S. अ+र+कः
def: Wrangling, altercation, row, pother; entanglement, complication, difficulty, dilemma, imbroglio, mess, &c. (syn. bakheṛā).

ety: S. झं+मेला and मेल+कः; or el (pleon.) = अडो (with euph. y inserted) = S. अ+र+कः
def: Wrangling, altercation, row, pother; entanglement, complication, difficulty, dilemma, imbroglio, mess, &c. (syn. bakheṛā).

ety: jhamel, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः(इन्+कः)
def: s.m. A wrangler, a quarrelsome fellow, a brawler; a mischief-maker, troublesome fellow, pest, &c. ( = bakheṛiyā).
s.m. & adv. The sound of heavy rain; dashing, beating (of rain), steady downpour; — heavily and continuously (raining).
s.f. Glitter, sparkle, &c. = jhalak, q.v. s.f. Brightness, radiance, glare, glitter, sparkle, lustre, splendour, refulgence, reflection (of light).
v.n. To shine, sparkle, flash, glitter, dance (as light).
adj. (f. -ī), Shining, glittering, brilliant, resplendent.
